alerts.title: Trump reportedly called off Tuesday Iran strike at Gulf allies' request
alerts.summary: PBS reports Trump confirmed he scrubbed a planned Tuesday strike against Iran after intervention from Gulf state partners, while NYT reports he continues to weigh new strike options. The disclosure indicates US forces were within hours of kinetic action and that Gulf capitals — not Israel — are now the decisive moderating voice in Washington's targeting cycle.

alerts.title: Khamenei sets uranium red line as Iran denies deal is imminent
alerts.summary: Reuters exclusive cites Iranian sources saying Supreme Leader Khamenei has insisted enriched uranium must remain inside Iran, directly contradicting the US position (per ynetnews) that Trump assured Israel no deal without dismantling the nuclear program. BBC reports Iran now says a deal is 'not imminent,' walking back Rubio's earlier Monday timeline as Trump tells negotiators 'not to rush.'

alerts.title: US deploying defensive 'umbrella' over Strait of Hormuz shipping
alerts.summary: DefenseScoop reports the US is fielding a layered 'umbrella' of defense and technology assets to shield commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, even as Trump claims a deal to reopen the strait is 'largely negotiated' (PBS). USS Florida (SSGN) remains forward-deployed in the Persian Gulf alongside three Red Sea destroyers, indicating posture is being reinforced, not drawn down, during talks.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes kill medics in Lebanon; Hezbollah affirms Iran will not abandon it
alerts.summary: NYT and Guardian report Israeli bombardment of southern Lebanon killed medics and reduced buildings to craters despite the existing fragile truce, while France 24 reports Hezbollah publicly declared Iran 'will not abandon' the group. The strikes plus the Iran-Hezbollah signaling raise the risk of a second-front complication just as US-Iran negotiations enter their most sensitive phase.

alerts.title: Israel claims it destroyed Iran's cyber warfare headquarters
alerts.summary: Politico reports Israeli officials have announced the destruction of Iran's cyber warfare headquarters, a claim that — if accurate — represents a significant degradation of Tehran's offensive cyber capability against Gulf shipping and regional infrastructure. Coupled with Israeli officials calling the emerging US-Iran framework a 'bad deal' (Jerusalem Post), the disclosure appears timed to harden Washington's terms.

analysis_note: The US-Iran track is in a high-volatility holding pattern: Trump has confirmed a Tuesday strike was aborted at Gulf urging, yet defensive posture around Hormuz is being reinforced and Khamenei's uranium red line directly collides with US dismantlement demands — while Israeli kinetic and cyber actions appear designed to constrain any emerging deal.
